preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] (or the labialized glide [w]) into the voiced bilabial stop [b] is a form of consonantalization and fortition, documented in specific language families where vowels harden into labial consonants.

examples.note: Regular shift where historical rounded vowels or labialized glides fortified into bilabial stops (e.g., Bats 'eb lar' corresponding to Chechen 'uollan').
Correspondences between labial glides and bilabial stops in Germanic dialects (e.g., English 'seven' vs. German 'sieben').
